decks.notes: This deck aims to aggressively control the game with a mix of cheap removal spells and powerful threats. By efficiently managing resources and disrupting opponents with counterspells and land destruction, the deck can quickly close out games using evasive creatures like Dragon's Rage Channeler and Murktide Regent. The inclusion of versatile cards like Brazen Borrower and Hexing Squelcher allows for adaptability against a variety of opposing strategies. This deck thrives on tempo, capitalizing on early advantage to maintain pressure and secure victories in the Legacy 1 vs 1 format.

This Eldrazi Aggro deck focuses on quickly deploying powerful Eldrazi creatures, disrupting opponents with Chalice of the Void and Thought-Knot Seer, and controlling the battlefield with Wasteland and Wastescape Battlemage. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with early aggression and resilient threats, leveraging mana acceleration from Lotus Petal and Ancient Tomb. The inclusion of cards like Glaring Fleshraker and Reality Smasher ensures that the deck can maintain pressure and close out games quickly.

The main idea of this deck is to utilize a combination of disruptive spells and efficient creatures to control the pace of the game and apply pressure to the opponent. By using cards like Thoughtseize and Force of Will to disrupt the opponent's strategy, while also deploying threats like Nethergoyf and Barrowgoyf to quickly close out the game. The inclusion of cards like Moonshadow and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student provide card advantage and additional disruption, making it difficult for the opponent to recover and establishing dominance on the battlefield.

The main concept of this deck is to use the combo of Painter's Servant and Grindstone to mill the opponent's deck out. The deck also utilizes cards like Goblin Welder and Goblin Engineer to search for and recur key pieces, while also disrupting the opponent with counters like Pyroblast and Red Elemental Blast. The deck's fast mana sources like Ancient Tomb and Simian Spirit Guide help accelerate the combo, while cards like Karn, the Great Creator and Ensnaring Bridge provide additional control elements. The deck aims to quickly assemble the combo and disrupt the opponent's game plan to secure a victory.

The deck focuses on using powerful green creatures and mana acceleration to control the board and disrupt the opponent's strategy. By using cards like Gaea's Cradle and Green Sun's Zenith to quickly ramp up mana, the player can play big threats like Craterhoof Behemoth and Atraxa, Grand Unifier. Cards like Thoughtseize and Endurance provide disruptive elements to disrupt the opponent's game plan, while cards like Natural Order can quickly bring out game-winning creatures. The deck also takes advantage of cards like Wasteland to disrupt the opponent's mana base and gain an advantage on the board.

The deck focuses on disrupting your opponent's game plan while building up your own board presence with aggressive creatures. By utilizing cards like Thoughtseize, Swords to Plowshares, and Skyclave Apparition, you can control the board and remove threats. Stoneforge Mystic and Aether Vial help you cheat out powerful equipment and creatures, while cards like Recruiter of the Guard and Overlord of the Balemurk help you find the pieces you need to win. Yorion, Sky Nomad provides additional value by blinking your creatures and gaining card advantage. Overall, the strategy is to outmaneuver your opponent and overwhelm them with efficient creatures and disruption.

The deck focuses on quickly establishing board control and disrupting the opponent's strategy with a mix of cheap removal spells like Fatal Push and Hymn to Tourach, while also protecting its own threats with Counterspells like Force of Will. Murktide Regent and Kaito, Bane of Nightmares provide powerful finishers that can quickly close out the game. The deck also utilizes cards like Thoughtseize and Brainstorm to help sculpt its hand and disrupt the opponent's game plan, while also maintaining consistency with card draw options like Ponder and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student.

This Legacy 1 vs 1 Combo - Show and Tell deck focuses on quickly cheating powerful creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn into play using cards like Sneak Attack and Show and Tell. With Stock Up to protect key pieces and cards like Force of Will to disrupt the opponent's game plan, the deck aims to overwhelm opponents with big threats early in the game. Additional protection and card selection tools like Veil of Summer and Ponder help to ensure consistency in executing the combo. This deck thrives on its ability to rapidly deploy game-ending threats and control the pace of the game.

The core idea of this deck is to quickly ramp up mana using Cloudpost and other land cards, allowing the player to cast high-cost spells like Planar Nexus and Ugin, Eye of the Storms. The deck also includes combo pieces like Stock Up and Thundertrap Trainer to help draw cards and set up game-winning plays. Additionally, cards like Force of Negation and Force of Will provide protection against opposing spells, while Karn, the Great Creator can be used to fetch additional artifacts for added utility. The overall strategy revolves around building a powerful board presence and controlling the game through powerful spells and effects.

This UR Aggro Legacy deck focuses on aggressive play, utilizing cards like Dragon's Rage Channeler and Murktide Regent to quickly overwhelm opponents. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Daze and Force of Will to protect its threats and disrupt opponents' strategies. The deck's mana base is optimized with fetch lands and dual lands to ensure consistent casting of spells. Overall, this deck aims to apply pressure early on and maintain tempo throughout the game to secure a quick victory.

The primary idea of the deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and strong removal spells. The deck utilizes creatures like Guide of Souls, Orcish Bowmasters, and Voice of Victory to put pressure on the opponent while using cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Cabal Therapy to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The inclusion of cards like Swords to Plowshares and Thoughtseize helps to maintain board control and disrupt the opponent's strategy. Overall, the deck aims to establish early board dominance and apply pressure to secure a swift victory.

The deck focuses on assembling the Doomsday combo to win the game quickly. This involves manipulating your deck to put Thassa's Oracle on top, then casting Doomsday to draw and play Oracle for the win. The deck includes cards like Thoughtseize and Force of Will to protect the combo, along with cantrips like Ponder and Brainstorm to find the pieces you need. Key cards like Lotus Petal and Dark Ritual help generate mana quickly, while disruption like Daze and Wasteland help keep your opponent off balance while you set up your combo.

The deck aims to quickly combo off and win the game by milling the entire library of the opponent using cards like Balustrade Spy, Undercity Informer, and Boggart Trawler in combination with spells like Cabal Ritual and Dark Ritual. By utilizing rituals and mana acceleration, the deck can quickly empty its hand of cards and generate enough mana to play its key combo pieces. The inclusion of counter spells like Pact of Negation provides protection for the combo, while cards like Memory's Journey and Thassa's Oracle serve as alternate win conditions if the primary combo is disrupted.

The deck focuses on quickly generating mana, creating a storm count, and utilizing tutor effects to search for and assemble a powerful combo. It utilizes cards like Dark Ritual, Lion's Eye Diamond, and Wishclaw Talisman to generate mana and card advantage. The deck also includes a variety of tutor effects like Infernal Tutor and Burning Wish to search for key combo pieces like Ad Nauseam and Tendrils of Agony to win the game. The goal is to create a high storm count and cast a lethal Tendrils of Agony to drain the opponent's life total in one explosive turn.

This deck focuses on aggressively disrupting the opponent's strategy by utilizing hatebears and disruptive creatures like Skyclave Apparition and Stoneforge Mystic. It also has a strong mana denial plan with cards like Wasteland and Karakas to slow down the opponent's game plan. The deck aims to control the board while applying pressure with creatures like Overlord of the Balemurk and Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd. Additionally, the deck has a strong synergy with Aether Vial to cheat creatures into play and overwhelm the opponent.

The deck focuses on using lands as a resource to control the game, utilizing cards like Exploration, Life from the Loam, and Crop Rotation to manipulate the board state. The key strategy is to quickly assemble the Dark Depths/Thespian's Stage combo to create a powerful 20/20 flying indestructible creature, while also disrupting the opponent's mana base with Wasteland and Ghost Quarter. The deck has strong protection with cards like Bojuka Bog and The Tabernacle at Pendrell Vale, and can control the opponent's nonland permanents with Pithing Needle and Grafdigger's Cage.
